calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery
Off Calendar Jul-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association Inc.'s Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-29-2007 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-02-2007 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Court Trial this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, CSR: Trina Ayala.
Atty. Robert D. Resner, Esq. for Plaintiff: Creditor's Trade Association;
No appearance for the defendants Verdun Lyles Enterprise and Marvin Isiah Lyles.
Witness Ross Hawkins was sworn and examined for the plaintiff.
The following exhibits were identified and moved into evidence:
Exh. A- Personal Credit Application
Exh. B- Advertisement of the SF Chronicle

After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendants Verdun Lyles Enteprise and Marvin Isiah Lyles as follows:
Principal $7,650.00
Interest- $2,167.75
Cost- $300.00 for a total judgment of $10,117.75. Counsel to file Memo of Cost for Atty's fee. Counsel to prepare proposed judgment.

Counsel stipulated for the return of the exhibits.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2006 Set For Trial On Jan-29-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206
